raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased four vintage jewelry items from XXXX XXXX for {$620.00}. The sales associate told me one necklace was platinum, and both the tag ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and the handwritten invoice ( XXXX XXXX ) reinforced that impression. After the purchase, I confirmed the item is only silver-tone. This was a material misrepresentation that affected my decision to buy.

The store refused a refund. The owner stated the refund-policy sign was behind the counter, but during both of my visits no sign was visible from any customer position. The clerk stood in front of the counter and customers can not see the back wall. XXXX XXXX customer photos also show no visible refund policy. Under NY GBL 218-a, a refund policy must be clearly and conspicuously posted before purchase, which was not the case.

I emailed the merchant multiple times requesting a refund. They refused and became hostile. On XX/XX/XXXX, I returned to the store with all four items to attempt an in-person refund. The clerk again refused any refund and offered only store credit, and made inappropriate comments. The items remain unused and in original condition.

After the purchase, I also found one same necklace sold online for {$75.00} ( vs. {$210.00} at the store ), supporting my concern of misrepresentation and unfair pricing.

I disputed the charge with Citi. I submitted evidence, but Citi closed the dispute without explaining what evidence they reviewed, without providing the basis for their decision, and without giving me an opportunity to submit further documentation as required under the billing error resolution rules. Citis letter was a generic denial with no investigation details.

I am requesting CFPB assistance because : ( 1 ) the merchant misrepresented material information, ( 2 ) the store failed to disclose its refund policy in a legally required manner, ( 3 ) I attempted resolution multiple times without success, and ( 4 ) Citi did not properly investigate my dispute or provide the required explanation.

I am requesting a full refund upon return of all merchandise.
